metadata.task: Create joint TEP triptych from EXP04 stimulus condition data
metadata.context: Phantom study analyzing brain signal during TMS stimulation
metadata.previous_approach: EXP04 couldnt make a good image

problem_statement.issue: Current approach fails to follow import structure for EXP04 data
problem_statement.constraint: EXP04 has 1 stim file to use for this, splitted into pre and post-stim conditions
problem_statement.requirement: Adapt the TEP plot triptych logic to work with 1 stimulus data file

step_1.key_logic: Define title, output metric, and labels before plotting

step_2.description: Load and pad images to equal width

implementation.read_images: Use matplotlib.image.imread() to load PNG files

padding.calculate: max_width = max(img.shape[1] for img in imgs)
padding.pad_all: For each image, pad right side to match max_width
padding.padding_value: Determine from image dtype: 1.0 for float, 255 for uint8

implementation.stack: Use np.vstack() to combine all images

step_4.description: Create figure and render stacked image

step.task: Understand how EXP04 stimulus data is structured (nested pre/post within stim)
step.action: Examine raw_stim structure and extract pre_stim and post_stim subsets

step.task: Adapt data loading to EXP04 format
step.action: Load stimulus condition, partition into pre_stim and post_stim windows

step.task: Apply TEP pipeline (baseline, crop, filter, decay removal)
step.action: Replicate EXP04 logic on all 2 evoked responses
Save pre_stim, post_stim, plots with consistent styling
Implement image loading, padding, and stacking logic
Run script, check triptych visual alignment and quality

validation.test_1: Verify all 2 images pad to same width
validation.test_2: Confirm stacked image renders without distortion
validation.test_3: Check that padding color matches image background cleanly
validation.test_4: Validate output PNG dimensions are correct
